edges.description: IC1 gain of methylation and paternal UPD11 increase IGF2 dosage.
IC2 loss of methylation and CDKN1C variants reduce CDKN1C growth-suppressor activity.
Increased IGF2 growth signaling promotes overgrowth and embryonal tumor risk.
Loss of CDKN1C growth suppression contributes to overgrowth and tumor predisposition.

edges.source: Tumor surveillance (abdominal ultrasound and serum AFP)
Tumor surveillance (abdominal ultrasound and serum AFP)

nodes.description: The initiating lesion in BWS is a genetic or epigenetic alteration of the imprinted 11p15.5 domain (IC1 gain of methylation, IC2 loss of methylation, paternal uniparental disomy, copy-number variants, or CDKN1C variants) that disrupts parent-of-origin regulation of the growth promoter IGF2 and the growth suppressor CDKN1C.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular Basis of Beckwith-Wiedemann Syndrome Spectrum with Associated Tumors and Consequences for Clinical Practice.
evidence.snippet: The tumor risk of up to 30% depends on the molecular subtype of BWSp with causative genetic or epigenetic alterations in the chromosomal region 11p15.5.

meta.association: Maternally expressed growth suppressor; loss-of-function variants cause BWS

evidence.reference_title: Phenotype, cancer risk, and surveillance in Beckwith-Wiedemann syndrome depending on molecular genetic subgroups.
evidence.snippet: In the CDKN1C subgroup 2.8% of patients developed neuroblastoma.

nodes.description: IC2 loss of methylation permits biallelic KCNQ1OT1 expression and reduces expression of the maternally expressed cyclin-dependent kinase inhibitor CDKN1C; maternal CDKN1C loss-of-function variants have the same effect. Loss of this growth brake removes negative regulation of cell proliferation.

biological_processes: negative regulation of cell population proliferation

evidence.reference_title: Phenotype, cancer risk, and surveillance in Beckwith-Wiedemann syndrome depending on molecular genetic subgroups.
evidence.snippet: Hepatoblastoma occurred mostly in the pUPD (3.5%) and IC2 (0.7%) subgroups, never in the IC1 and CDKN1C subgroups, and always before 30 months of age.

meta.association: Maternally expressed lncRNA within the IC1 imprinted region

evidence.reference_title: Phenotype, cancer risk, and surveillance in Beckwith-Wiedemann syndrome depending on molecular genetic subgroups.
evidence.snippet: Tumor risks were highest in the IC1 (H19/IGF2:IG-DMR) hypermethylation subgroup (28%)

nodes.description: Hepatoblastoma is a cardinal embryonal liver tumor of BWS, occurring mostly in the pUPD and IC2 subgroups and almost always before 30 months of age; cooperating CTNNB1 (Wnt/beta-catenin) variants are essentially universal.

evidence.reference_title: Phenotype, cancer risk, and surveillance in Beckwith-Wiedemann syndrome depending on molecular genetic subgroups.
evidence.snippet: Hepatoblastoma occurred mostly in the pUPD (3.5%) and IC2 (0.7%) subgroups, never in the IC1 and CDKN1C subgroups, and always before 30 months of age.

evidence.reference_title: Occurrence of Hepatoblastomas in Patients with Beckwith-Wiedemann Spectrum (BWSp).
evidence.snippet: Patients with Beckwith-Wiedemann syndrome (BWS), an epigenetic imprinting disorder involving alterations in genes at the 11p15 chromosomal location, are predisposed to develop hepatoblastomas (HBs), which are rare embryonal liver tumors.

nodes.description: A subset of infants have persistent hypoglycemia due to hyperinsulinism requiring endocrinologic management.

evidence.reference_title: Beckwith-Wiedemann Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: in some cases, persistent hypoglycemia due to hyperinsulinism may require consultation with an endocrinologist for therapeutic intervention.

nodes.description: Neonatal hypoglycemia is treated with oral feeding when mild or glucose supplementation; hyperinsulinism is treated with standard pharmacotherapy per endocrinologist, and partial pancreatectomy may be considered in persistent hypoglycemia unresponsive to medical treatment.

evidence.reference_title: Beckwith-Wiedemann Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Hyperinsulinism is treated with standard pharmacotherapy per endocrinologist; partial pancreatectomy may be considered in those with persistent hypoglycemia who are unresponsive to pharmacologic treatment.

meta.association: Imprinted growth promoter overexpressed in IC1 GoM and pUPD11

evidence.reference_title: Phenotype, cancer risk, and surveillance in Beckwith-Wiedemann syndrome depending on molecular genetic subgroups.
evidence.snippet: Tumor risks were highest in the IC1 (H19/IGF2:IG-DMR) hypermethylation subgroup (28%) and pUPD subgroup (16%)

nodes.description: IC1 gain of methylation and paternal uniparental disomy of 11p15 increase expression of the paternally expressed insulin-like growth factor IGF2 (with reduced H19), driving excessive fetal and tissue growth and contributing to Wilms tumor predisposition.

biological_processes: insulin-like growth factor receptor signaling pathway

evidence.reference_title: Phenotype, cancer risk, and surveillance in Beckwith-Wiedemann syndrome depending on molecular genetic subgroups.
evidence.snippet: Tumor risks were highest in the IC1 (H19/IGF2:IG-DMR) hypermethylation subgroup (28%) and pUPD subgroup (16%)

meta.association: Paternally expressed lncRNA; biallelic in IC2 loss of methylation

evidence.reference_title: Phenotype, cancer risk, and surveillance in Beckwith-Wiedemann syndrome depending on molecular genetic subgroups.
evidence.snippet: the KCNQ1OT1:TSS-DMR (IC2) subgroup (2.6%)

nodes.description: Enlarged tongue is a cardinal feature, observed in roughly 90% of BWSp patients, and may obstruct breathing or feeding.

evidence.reference_title: Beckwith-Wiedemann Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Macroglossia is generally present at birth and can obstruct breathing or interfere with feeding in infants.

nodes.description: Wilms tumor is a cardinal embryonal tumor of BWS, most frequent in the IC1 and pUPD subgroups (median age at diagnosis ~24 months), with risk concentrated in the first seven years of life.

evidence.reference_title: Phenotype, cancer risk, and surveillance in Beckwith-Wiedemann syndrome depending on molecular genetic subgroups.
evidence.snippet: Wilms tumors (median age 24 months) were frequent in the IC1 (24%) and pUPD (7.9%) subgroups.

nodes.description: Abdominal wall defect (exomphalos) is one of the classic triad features of BWS.

evidence.reference_title: Beckwith-Wiedemann Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Beckwith-Wiedemann syndrome (BWS) is a growth disorder variably characterized by macroglossia, hemihyperplasia, omphalocele, neonatal hypoglycemia, macrosomia, embryonal tumors

nodes.description: Standard surgical treatment is recommended for omphalocele per pediatric surgeon.

evidence.reference_title: Beckwith-Wiedemann Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Standard treatment is recommended for omphalocele per pediatric surgeon.

nodes.description: Combined IGF2 excess and CDKN1C loss promote increased proliferation and a proposed stalling of cellular differentiation in developing organs, predisposing persisting embryonic cells to accumulate additional defects and develop embryonal tumors (notably Wilms tumor and hepatoblastoma, in which cooperating Wnt/CTNNB1 activation is frequent).

biological_processes: negative regulation of cell population proliferation

evidence.reference_title: Molecular Basis of Beckwith-Wiedemann Syndrome Spectrum with Associated Tumors and Consequences for Clinical Practice.
evidence.snippet: The molecular basis of tumor formation appears to relate to stalled cellular differentiation in certain organs that predisposes persisting embryonic cells to accumulate additional molecular defects, which then results in a range of embryonal tumors.

evidence.reference_title: Occurrence of Hepatoblastomas in Patients with Beckwith-Wiedemann Spectrum (BWSp).
evidence.snippet: We found that 100% of the HBs that underwent NGS panel testing had variants in the CTNNB1 gene.

evidence.reference_title: Cancer predisposition signaling in Beckwith-Wiedemann Syndrome drives Wilms tumor development.
evidence.snippet: CTNNB1 overexpression and broad range of interactions were seen in the BWS-WT interactome study.

nodes.description: Macroglossia causing airway obstruction or feeding difficulty may require feeding and/or respiratory support, and tongue reduction surgery may be considered for functional and other indications.

evidence.reference_title: Beckwith-Wiedemann Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Tongue reduction surgery may be considered for this and other indications.

nodes.description: For most BWS molecular subtypes, tumor screening consists of abdominal ultrasound (liver, adrenal glands, kidneys) every three months until age four years, followed by kidney ultrasound every three months from age four to seven years. Serum alpha-fetoprotein (AFP) is measured every three months until age four years to screen for hepatoblastoma. The CDKN1C subgroup is an exception in updated harmonized recommendations given its lower Wilms tumor/hepatoblastoma burden.
nodes.id: Tumor surveillance (abdominal ultrasound and serum AFP)

evidence.reference_title: Beckwith-Wiedemann Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: For most BWS molecular subtypes, tumor screening consists of abdominal ultrasound with views of the liver, adrenal glands, and kidneys every three months until age four years followed by kidney ultrasound only every three months from age four to seven years. Serum alpha-fetoprotein (AFP) levels are performed every three months until age four years.

evidence.reference_title: Update on Surveillance for Wilms Tumor and Hepatoblastoma in Beckwith-Wiedemann Syndrome and Other Predisposition Syndromes.
evidence.snippet: members of the American Association for Cancer Research Pediatric Cancer Working Group met to update those guidelines with a review of more recently published evidence and risk estimates
